setting.note: The abbey steps and the harbour at Whitby, where the Demeter runs aground, are exact and still walkable. Transylvania is drawn from maps Stoker never travelled and Carfax is invented, which is precisely what the labels are for.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Bram Stoker, first published 1897; the author died in 1912.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States and the United Kingdom, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1912).
rights.attribution: Bram Stoker, Dracula (1897). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: lips were crimson with fresh blood
palette.note: The blood-crimson of the Count, taken straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

mina.name: Mina Murray
mina.colorSourceQuote: the grey of the coming dawn
